SWAPNA
AUGUSTINE

Swapna Augustine is one of Kerala's most acclaimed artists, known for creating paintings using her legs as she was born without hands.

About the Artist & Challenges

Born in Muvattupuzha, Swapna discovered her love for colors and painting at the age of four. She began experimenting with watercolors and later moved on to acrylic and oil paintings. Although she completed her graduation in History, she eventually chose painting as her full-time career. Her family also played an important role in supporting and encouraging her artistic journey from childhood.

What makes Swapna's journey even more remarkable is that she had no formal training in art during the early stages of her career. Her talent later gained recognition from the Association of Mouth and Foot Painting Artists, where she received guidance and encouragement from experts.

Swapna has created thousands of artworks and earned appreciation from audiences across different platforms. She was recently recognized for her works during an event in Goa and has received several awards for her artistic achievements. Apart from painting, she also spends time teaching children and wishes to share her creativity and skills with others.

Inspiration and Message

Swapna believes that positivity and confidence have been the key to her success. Through her journey, she continues to inspire many people by proving that passion and determination are stronger than physical limitations.

She encourages people with disabilities to step out into the world, explore opportunities, and believe in themselves rather than limiting their lives within fear or hesitation.

Inspiring Quote
"There are many opportunities out there that you must go and grab."

Her life and artistic journey stand as a powerful example of courage, perseverance, and self-belief.

Theme of the Painting: Pooram Festival

This painting beautifully portrays the grandeur and cultural spirit of Pooram, one of the most celebrated temple festivals in Kerala. Pooram festivals are famous for decorated elephants, traditional percussion music, colorful umbrellas, and vibrant processions that reflect Kerala's rich traditions and heritage.

The artwork captures the festive atmosphere through the majestic elephant, temple background, and traditional musicians performing during the procession. The painting reflects devotion, celebration, and the cultural beauty associated with Kerala's Pooram festivals while showcasing the artistic talent of Swapna Augustine.

SWAPNA AUGUSTINE
